Document:
[color=#ffff00][head=3]Section 10: Escalation Guildelines[/head][/color]
[color=#a4885c]10.1[/color] Escalate conflicts reasonably. Use common sense when deciding how far a situation should go. A spilled drink might lead to an argument or fistfight, but should not escalate into a gunfight. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
[color=#a4885c]10.2[/color] When instigating follow these steps: Talk and posture, this can include drawing a weapon. Threaten with violence. Fire a warning shot if possible. ★
- [color=#a4885c]10.2A[/color] If you are Command, Security, TDF, or a Self-Antag, you are expected to have and attempt to use some form of non-lethal force before resorting to lethal force. If you are none of those roles and have access to non-lethal options, you should still attempt to use them.
- [color=#a4885c]10.2B[/color] If someone boards your ship, escalation rules are more lenient for the purpose of removing them from your ship.
- [color=#a4885c]10.2C[/color] If you attempt to coerce someone through threats of escalation, and they refuse, you can treat that as consent to escalate further. Threats of escalation must be follow rule 10.1, for example "Drop your gun or I'll shoot you", not "Empty your bank account or I'll max cap you".
- [color=#a4885c]10.2D[/color] Defenders of aggression can escalate and set the amount of escalation for purposes of protecting themselves.
[color=#a4885c]10.3[/color] If someone is surrendering, you must accept it, no matter how heated a situation is. Surrendering is an 'opt-out' for PvP interactions, please talk in LOOC after a surrender, to determine what would be a preferred outcome to the scene. ★
- [color=#a4885c]10.3A[/color] Do not intentionally offer a false surrender.
[color=#a4885c]10.4[/color] Do not intentionally round-remove Characters. If you kill someone, you are not expected to revive them, but you must not take any actions that would prevent Medical from reviving them. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
- [color=#a4885c]10.4A[/color] Emotional Support Pets, and Borgs also apply to this role when selected as Ghost Roles. Other Ghost roles such as antagonistic ghost roles or vermin animals (mice, mothroaches) are exempt and can be round-removed.
